subject: Databases
title: INSERT Adds Rows
description: A quick, practical guide to INSERT Adds Rows.

hook.text: INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ('Ada', 'ada@test.com') — one new row, added in milliseconds.

buildup.text: List the columns and their values. The database validates types and constraints before saving.

discovery.text: Bulk inserts are faster: INSERT INTO users VALUES (...), (...), (...). One statement, many rows.

twist.text: Forget a NOT NULL column? The database rejects the insert. Constraints protect data quality.

climax.text: RETURNING (in Postgres) gives you the new row back, including any auto-generated ID. No second query needed.

punchline.text: INSERT adds data. Constraints keep it clean.

quiz.question: Why are bulk inserts faster than individual ones?
options: They reduce round trips and overhead by sending multiple rows in one statement
Bulk inserts skip the write-ahead log
